steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best places the 1821 Declaration in the context of Valle's changing political beliefs. The answer must connect three things: his initial stance against independence, what changed his mind, and his authorship of the declaration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A change in Valle's political beliefs that occurred when the Spanish king repealed the 1812 constitution led to Valle writing Central America's Declaration of Independence.: Fails to establish Valle's prior opposition to independence, so there is no 'change' to contextualize. ✗
  • Choice B — Long an opponent of Central American independence, Valle changed his mind after an 1820 revolt and wrote the 1821 declaration.: Describes Valle as 'long an opponent of Central American independence,' identifies the 1820 revolt as what changed his mind, and states he 'wrote the 1821 declaration'—all three required pieces. ✓
  • Choice C — The writing of Central America's Declaration of Independence may not have happened were it not for Colonel Riego's 1820 revolt.: Bypasses Valle's beliefs entirely and does not show any shift in his views. ✗
  • Choice D — Colonel Riego's revolt was the inspiration that Valle, a long-standing opponent of Central American independence, needed to change his political beliefs.: Identifies the 1820 revolt as the catalyst for Valle's changed beliefs but does not connect that change to the 1821 declaration or credit Valle with writing it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
